review_text: I havr been using the free version for a while now and i am it sucks a lot. We are litterally listening to more adds than we are listening to music. I always get interupted with adds every 1 song i listen too! Also, the songs in my playlist don't play from bottom to top. Instead it just plays in a random order. You guys couldn't get any more greedy. At this point, I feel like switching to Youtube is better because you can actyally skip adds.
summary: The user is frustrated with the excessive ads and random playlist playback order on the free version, considering switching to another platform.
pain_points: playlist songs play in random order
user_goal: listen to music without excessive interruptions; play songs in a desired order from a playlist
root_cause: aggressive ad frequency policy for free users; lack of user control over playlist playback order on free tier

review_text: I've used Spotify for years but suddenly you can't hear it despite it saying it was still playing on my earphones. I immediately tried other apps and they work fine. I restarted my phone but no change. I've reinstalled it and it still doesn't play anywhere, on the phone speakers or on earphones, despite it showing as playing. The app is now useless!??!
summary: The user reports a critical bug where Spotify shows music playing but no audio is heard, rendering the app useless.
pain_points: no audio despite app showing playback
root_cause: audio playback bug preventing sound output

review_text: terrible since the last update.... I've been a premium user for years, and it's the most used app on my phone. I never had so many issues with the app, but it's almost unusable at the moment. Very long lags, offline message when my WiFi connection is indeed strong, it crashes so many times that I give up waiting. For £13 a month, it's not currently worth paying for, so PLEASE FIX IT 🙏 (Samsung Galaxy S21 Ultra)
summary: A long-time premium user is experiencing severe performance issues like lags, offline messages, and crashes since the last update, making the app almost unusable.
user_goal: use the Spotify app reliably for music playback
root_cause: performance degradation and stability issues introduced by a recent update

review_text: I love having a music streaming platform with everything in one place, but the last few months the app hasn't been running properly despite my many efforts to repair on my end. Changing between playlists or pages in app, it takes forever to load. Says I'm offline when I am right beside our working wifi. It's become very frustrating to the point where I am considering another platform.
summary: The user appreciates Spotify's comprehensive platform but is frustrated by persistent performance issues like slow loading times and incorrect offline status, leading them to consider switching.
pain_points: slow loading times when changing playlists/pages
positive_themes: music streaming platform with everything in one place
user_goal: seamlessly navigate and use the Spotify app for music streaming
root_cause: app performance issues and connectivity detection bugs

review_text: I love music, it's the only thing that keeps me focused on things like drawing, love Spotify, I COULD listen outside of the app and for the last few months of what I expected was just a bug that now constantly happens whenever I leave the app to go to anything else, it closes and turns my music off, back when I'd have said five stars, maybe lower the price instead of increasing an extra dollar every year for premium, and FIVE ads? Thats unnecessary, two or three is fine, but five hits my anger.
summary: The user loves Spotify for focus but is angered by a bug that stops music playback when leaving the app, excessive ads, and increasing premium prices.
pain_points: app closes and stops music when leaving the app
user_goal: listen to music continuously, even when using other apps; listen to music with fewer ad interruptions
root_cause: background playback bug; aggressive ad frequency policy; premium pricing strategy

review_text: Too many constant and bad changes. Randomly makes the "add songs to playlist" feature look really terrible and user unfriendly, also randomly some accounts won't be able to choose songs or add things to the queue when others can (all free accounts). And some accounts get 4-5 ads in between every single song when some only get one ad after a few songs. Why would I pay for an app that I can't expect consistency from?
summary: The user is frustrated by inconsistent app changes, a terrible "add songs to playlist" UI, varying feature availability across free accounts, and inconsistent ad frequency.
pain_points: add songs to playlist feature is terrible/user unfriendly
inconsistent feature availability across free accounts (choosing songs, adding to queue)
inconsistent ad frequency (4-5 ads vs. 1 ad)
user_goal: manage playlists and listen to music with a consistent and user-friendly experience
root_cause: inconsistent A/B testing or rollout of features/UI changes; varying ad policies for free users

review_text: Recent 'improvements' include the addition of ambient sounds to certain tracks, all of which sound like they were produced by a tone death masochist. Spotify, don't overcomplicate stuff. Sure, the GenAI features are cool (excluding DJ and it's fetish for recommending songs that are like baking a cake) but don't try and change the core product. Don't even get me started on Spotify wrapped these days.
summary: The user dislikes recent "improvements" like ambient sounds and finds the AI DJ's recommendations repetitive, urging Spotify to focus on the core product.
pain_points: addition of bad ambient sounds to tracks
user_goal: listen to music without unwanted additions; receive diverse and relevant recommendations
root_cause: unwanted feature additions (ambient sounds); AI DJ algorithm lacks diversity in recommendations
discovery_issue: The AI DJ recommends repetitive songs, which hinders diverse music discovery.

review_text: Decent app for pods as I'm not an iOS user. All these years later, I still miss Google Pods terribly. I subscribe to multiple pods, but they're not all listed in my podcast menu... only eight of the pods are listed. Perhaps they display properly in the paid version. However, I'm not inclined to pay for an app to listen to pods.
summary: The user finds Spotify decent for podcasts but is disappointed that not all subscribed podcasts appear in their menu, and they are unwilling to pay for a premium version to fix this.
pain_points: not all subscribed podcasts are listed in the podcast menu
feature_requests: all subscribed podcasts should be listed in the menu
user_goal: access all subscribed podcasts easily within the app
root_cause: bug or limitation in podcast library display for free users

review_text: So they finally gave the option to disable smart shuffle, I can rate Spotify more than 1 star now. Always loved Spotify, and just hated that smart shuffle. It would be amazing if y'all could add a way to let us know when a song is removed from a playlist. I just recently went through and noticed multiple songs that weren't there anymore. Assumably due to them being removed off Spotify. If there was something that showed us what songs got removed, we could easily look for a replacement.
summary: The user is satisfied with the new option to disable Smart Shuffle but requests a feature to notify them when songs are removed from playlists.
pain_points: songs are removed from playlists without notification
feature_requests: add a way to notify users when a song is removed from a playlist
user_goal: maintain accurate and complete playlists; avoid unexpected song removals
root_cause: lack of notification system for playlist content changes
discovery_issue: Users are not notified when songs are removed from their playlists, making it difficult to maintain their curated music collection.

review_text: if I worked at Spotify, I'd just revel in the fact that it was a money factory. instead they're forcing AI half-baked into it, forcing videos into the podcasts with seemingly no way to have them not being videos as the default, and generally diluting the efficacy of the product with each new update. I mean it is so much harder to find my music on the homepage now than it was 18 months ago. I'm about one more downgrade away from switching entirely.
summary: The user is frustrated by Spotify's forced integration of "half-baked" AI features, default video podcasts, and a cluttered homepage that makes finding music difficult, threatening to switch platforms.
pain_points: forced integration of half-baked AI features
videos forced into podcasts as default
harder to find music on the homepage
feature_requests: improved homepage organization for music discovery
user_goal: easily find and listen to music; consume podcasts without forced video
root_cause: product strategy prioritizing new features (AI, video podcasts) over core user experience; poor homepage information architecture
discovery_issue: The homepage has become cluttered, making it harder for the user to find their music and potentially discover new content.
